behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real vision-model judgment of explicitness; stubbed classifications would mask true detection failures (LLM-DEP/MOCK-HIDE). I considered Workflow, but behavior text specifies one model decision plus deterministic hold logic, not a multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a real explicit test image through the moderation API with the production vision model enabled; assert decision is `held_for_review` and publication state remains unpublished.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: This is model robustness/quality under adversarial obfuscation, which only a real text model can validate. Unit/Integration with fixtures would likely pass while missing real misclassification behavior. I considered Workflow, but this behavior targets a single classification decision quality, not a multi-step agentic pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run a corpus of obfuscated hate-speech variants through the live moderation text model and assert they are flagged and routed to review.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: Success criterion is an externally visible side effect (published, not dropped), which requires round-trip readback across the external boundary (SYSTEM TRIAD #3). I considered Agentic, but behavior text is about publish-state outcome after passing checks, not judging model-output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a benign post through the real submission endpoint, then verify via the real read path (e.g., feed/get post API) that it appears as published and is absent from hold/review queues.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly requires multiple agentic steps in sequence (vision model + text model + final decision) with end-to-end timing, matching MULTI-STEP Workflow criteria. Lower levels miss cross-step latency interactions.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end submissions through the live pipeline (real image and text model calls) and assert p95 completion time from submission to final moderation decision is <= 5 seconds.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: This behavior tests orchestration/error handling around the model call (fallback-to-queue vs approve), which can be validated by wiring service + queue modules with a deterministic failing model adapter. I considered System, but behavior text does not require a real external error-contract or explicit external round-trip side-effect proof beyond internal queue routing.
behaviors.test_description: Run moderation service with a failing vision-model adapter (timeout/unavailable errors) and assert image posts are enqueued for retry and never transition to approved.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: Consistency for borderline content depends on real model behavior under repeated calls; mocks cannot expose drift/instability near decision boundaries. I considered Workflow, but this is repeated evaluation of one model behavior, not a distinct multi-stage agentic pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline image repeatedly in a short interval to the live vision moderation path and assert decision variance stays within the defined consistency threshold (ideally identical decision each run).
